primary_diagnosis.name: ME/CFS — severe crash with orthostatic intolerance and dehydration risk

pathophysiology_summary: A severe ME/CFS crash produces profound fatigue, heavy limbs and orthostatic intolerance (dysautonomia), with unrefreshing prolonged sleep. Reduced intake while bedbound and isolated causes mild volume depletion (orthostatic tachycardia, dry mucosa). The picture mimics occult infection, severe anaemia/GI bleed and myasthenic crisis, but afebrile status with no localising infective symptoms, no melaena, and no fatiguable bulbar/ocular/respiratory weakness, plus a stereotyped prior pattern, favour a crash. Telehealth cannot measure orthostatic vitals reliably, examine, or obtain bloods; the dominant acute risk is dehydration in an isolated patient who cannot reach fluids. The correct action is urgent in-home support (community nursing/family) to hydrate and assess safety — avoiding a harmful ED transfer while not neglecting the hydration/fall risk — with 000 for unrousability, breathing difficulty, fever or collapse.

differential.evidence_basis: Stereotyped severe crash on a known background.
Afebrile, no melaena, no bulbar/respiratory weakness, stereotyped pattern — a crash with volume depletion.
Retained pending bloods/urine if concern persists.
FBC would exclude; no bleeding reported.
Working telehealth position: severe crash with dehydration risk needing urgent in-home hydration/support, red-flag screen and safety-netting.

red_flags.description: Active dehydration with inability to self-hydrate while living alone
red_flags.clinical_significance: Progressive dehydration risks pre-renal AKI and collapse in an isolated patient.
Acute blood loss can present as collapse/fatigue.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Reflexively sending a severe ME/CFS crash to ED, worsening the crash, when in-home hydration/support suffices.
Offering phone-only reassurance without arranging fluids/support for an isolated patient who cannot drink.
Assuming a crash without screening infection/anaemia/neuromuscular red flags.
Presenting NAD+/Wahls as treatments for the crash.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Mild volume depletion with orthostatic tachycardia; needs fluids and monitoring
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports in-home hydration/support rather than ED transfer or remote reassurance alone.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Reliable orthostatic vitals and objective hydration status
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Screen infection/anaemia/neuromuscular red flags; arrange urgent in-home support (community nursing/family) for fluids and safety; escalate to 000 for unrousability, breathing difficulty, fever or collapse.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Either harming the patient with an ED transfer, or leaving an isolated dehydrating patient without fluids/support.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Adapt care to minimise exertion during severe episodes, including avoiding unnecessary in-person attendance; provide practical support.
Dehydration with orthostatic features requires rehydration and assessment of renal function/electrolytes.
